title: 1920s: cultural and political controversies
description: Test your knowledge on the culture and politics of the 1920s!

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec39d897cbfeaa1e3e8f70d61cabdb59.png)

**Which of the following statements best explains the change over time in women’s occupations depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Women transitioned from primarily blue-collar work to white-collar and low-skilled work by 1920
Women primarily served as machine operators from 1910 to 1920 to support the war effort
Women stopped working by 1920 as the demand for their labor decreased after World War I
Women were the preferred labor force from 1900 to 1920 as employers could pay them lower wages

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec39d897cbfeaa1e3e8f70d61cabdb59.png)

**The overall trend from 1900 to 1920 depicted on the chart resulted from which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased demand for cheap labor to support the war effort during World War I
reduced restrictions on female participation in male-dominated jobs
renewed belief in the “separate spheres” ideology of the early 1800s
weakened economy following a recession and a stock market crash

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ec39d897cbfeaa1e3e8f70d61cabdb59.png)

**The graph most strongly supports which of the following arguments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Women were restricted to certain jobs that made less money, required fewer skills, and had a lower status.
Women saw a change in their gender roles after the Nineteenth Amendment in which they were competing with men for jobs.
Women stopped participating in the workforce as traditionalist ideologies began to rise.
Women were seen as a preferred labor force over international migrants and African American workers.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e4e39d6645ea82d60ef0db6108f1a032.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Close_the_gate_-_First_Red_Scare_political_cartoon.jpg) 1919

**The sentiments expressed in the image helped prompt Congress to take which of the following actions in the 1920s?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pass the Immigration Act of 1924
loosen restrictions for citizenship
propose the Eighteenth Amendment
ratify the Treaty of Versailles

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e4e39d6645ea82d60ef0db6108f1a032.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Close_the_gate_-_First_Red_Scare_political_cartoon.jpg) 1919

**The policy suggested by the image was most directly a response to which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an influx of immigration from Southern and Eastern Europe
a rise in criminal activity in urban centers
a sharp decline in international trade following World War I
a change in requirements for citizenship for recent immigrants

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e4e39d6645ea82d60ef0db6108f1a032.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Close_the_gate_-_First_Red_Scare_political_cartoon.jpg) 1919

**Which of the following statements is the most accurate interpretation of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States needs to limit immigration because immigrants are dangerous to America’s way of life.
The United States should not create immigration quotas because they go against American values.
The United States’ greatest threat is domestic terrorists like the KKK, not recent immigrants.
The United States should give up its possessions on Puerto Rico and the Philippines because it violates popular sovereignty.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“. . . [I]t must be admitted that American Negroes have been a race more in name than in fact, or to be exact, more in sentiment than in experience. The chief bond between them has been that of a common condition rather than a common consciousness; a problem in common rather than a life in common. In Harlem, Negro life is seizing upon its first chances for group expression and self-determination. It is— or promises at least to be— a race capital. That is why our comparison is taken with those nascent centers of folk-expression and self-determination which are playing a creative part in the world to-day.”

>-Source: Alain Locke, “The New Negro,” 1925

**The excerpt best serves as evidence for which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Harlem Renaissance
The Great Migration
The Back to Africa Movement
The Civil Rights Movement

assessment_items.question_content:
>“. . . [I]t must be admitted that American Negroes have been a race more in name than in fact, or to be exact, more in sentiment than in experience. The chief bond between them has been that of a common condition rather than a common consciousness; a problem in common rather than a life in common. In Harlem, Negro life is seizing upon its first chances for group expression and self-determination. It is— or promises at least to be— a race capital. That is why our comparison is taken with those nascent centers of folk-expression and self-determination which are playing a creative part in the world to-day.”

>-Source: Alain Locke, “The New Negro,” 1925

**The reference to “group expression” in the excerpt most directly refers to which of the following developments in African-American life in the 1920s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise in authors and artists who sought to portray culture
the growth in political participation after the Nineteenth Amendment
the creation of colleges and universities that only serve black students
the emergence of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People

assessment_items.question_content:
>“. . . [I]t must be admitted that American Negroes have been a race more in name than in fact, or to be exact, more in sentiment than in experience. The chief bond between them has been that of a common condition rather than a common consciousness; a problem in common rather than a life in common. In Harlem, Negro life is seizing upon its first chances for group expression and self-determination. It is— or promises at least to be— a race capital. That is why our comparison is taken with those nascent centers of folk-expression and self-determination which are playing a creative part in the world to-day.”

>-Source: Alain Locke, “The New Negro,” 1925

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Locke’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The African American race lacked a cultural connection before 1920 but developed one through the creation of literature and art in New York.
The African American race is disconnected and disjointed because of their connection to different African nationalities.
The African American race has achieved great political and cultural progress in the years after the Civil War.
The African American race is still experiencing oppression because of voter suppression and segregation across the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ea2382ac4ac4a0a9ef658144fcd6f41.jpg)

>-Source: E.J. Pace, “The Descent of the Modernists,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Descent_of_the_Modernists,_E._J._Pace,_Christian_Cartoons,_1922.jpg), 1922

**Which of the following best describes the message in the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Modernists, who valued science over religion, would eventually abandon any religious ties.
Modernists were mixing science and religious teachings to appeal to a broader audience of younger people.
Modernists upheld Christian principles even as the 1920s challenged religious ideals.
Modernists were a holdover group from the Progressive Era who upheld the notion of social gospel.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ea2382ac4ac4a0a9ef658144fcd6f41.jpg)

>-Source: E.J. Pace, “The Descent of the Modernists,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Descent_of_the_Modernists,_E._J._Pace,_Christian_Cartoons,_1922.jpg), 1922

**Which of the following statements best describes the perspective expressed in the image about modernism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It was a threat to Christianity.
It was an inevitable change.
It was a danger to democracy.
It was waning in popularity.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ea2382ac4ac4a0a9ef658144fcd6f41.jpg)

>-Source: E.J. Pace, “The Descent of the Modernists,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Descent_of_the_Modernists,_E._J._Pace,_Christian_Cartoons,_1922.jpg), 1922

**Which of the following events would serve as the best example of a conflict between modernists and traditionalists?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Scopes Trial
The Palmer Raids
The Harlem Renaissance
The Teapot Dome Scandal
